S0001.title: Arrow, K. J. (1951). Social Choice and Individual Values

C0001.initial_statement: Approval voting prevents minority tyranny because it allows expressing support for multiple candidates

updates.update: Approval voting may not fully prevent gerrymandering
updates.reasoning: Approval voting solves some issues but not all
updates.inspiration: Review of Lijphart's democratic peace research

evidence_supporting: Approval voting allows voters to support multiple candidates, preventing vote-splitting

P0001.prompt: Analyze voting systems for minority protection

steps.content:
Research Question: What voting methods prevent minority tyranny?
Reasoning: Based on Arrow's Impossibility Theorem and need for fair representation
Discovered source: Arrow, K. J. (1951). Social Choice and Individual Values (https://www.wiley.com/en-us/Social+Choice+and+Individual+Values-p-9780300013205)
Extracted from S0001: Arrow's Impossibility Theorem: No rank-order system can satisfy all fairness criteria
Initial conjecture: Approval voting prevents minority tyranny because it allows expressing support for multiple candidates
Added supporting evidence to C0001: Approval voting allows voters to support multiple candidates, preventing vote-splitting
Conjecture C0001 updated: Approval voting may not fully prevent gerrymandering
Prompt 1: Analyze voting systems for minority protection...
Prompt 2: Evaluate approval voting vs ranked choice...

Final Conclusion: Approval voting is the best system for preventing minority tyranny
Confidence: 85%
